---
title: "Research"
---

My research asks how landscapes record interacting tectonic and climatic processes.

## Faults, earthquakes, and landscape response

Slowly slipping faults can leave measurable records in topography, drainage networks, sediment deposits, and surface deformation.

## Climate variability and sediment transport

I use landscape-evolution models to examine how climate oscillations and sediment cover influence rivers, drainage divides, and fault-zone morphology.

## Thermochronology and exhumation

I use low-temperature thermochronology and geologic constraints to reconstruct cooling, exhumation, and basin development.

## Glacial and postglacial landscapes

My developing research expands these questions into mountain systems shaped by ice, rivers, tectonics, and changing sediment flux.
